response.justification: Mohammed Al-Said is an experienced full-stack and web engineer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position requires native-level Japanese proficiency (an absolute hard requirement), deep C programming for embedded/UNIX development, Linux kernel-level expertise, and domain knowledge in network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Layer-2/3 switching) — none of which the candidate demonstrates. His listed languages are English, Romanian, and basic German, and his C exposure is minimal and dated (a 2014–2015 role and a bachelor thesis). There is no evidence of embedded systems, networking hardware, or security feature development.
strengths: Long and consistent software engineering career (15+ years) demonstrating adaptability across multiple stacks and domains
Some embedded-adjacent experience at National Instruments writing C/C++ components and at Altom working with hardware (robotic arms)
Familiarity with Linux environments and low-latency systems (DLang async I/O work at Dunnhumby)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — this is a stated hard requirement and is an immediate disqualifier
C is not a meaningful part of the candidate's skill set; the resume is dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and web technologies with no embedded networking work
Zero demonstrated knowledge of network security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, L2/L3 switching, NETCONF, SNMP) or Linux kernel internals

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
